Its self-evolving capabilities include a dynamic skills registry and a tool for creating new Python skills at runtime, along with a local RAG system for document analysis and retrieval.

chunks.text:
IP Prime Desktop Assistant - Architecture Guide

Chapter 1: Overview
IP Prime is a voice-controlled desktop assistant built with Python 3.11 and Google Generative AI.
It uses a modular architecture with separate components for planning, execution, memory, and skills management.
The assistant can control the computer, browse the web, manage files, play games, and much more.

Chapter 2: Memory System
The memory system uses a hierarchical JSON storage approach. Long-term memories are stored in
category-based JSON files under the memory/long_term/ directory. Each category (preferences, notes,
facts, context, skills) has its own file. This allows for virtually unlimited memory storage
without hitting single-file size limits.

Chapter 3: Skills and Tools
IP Prime has a dynamic skills registry that scans both core tools and dynamically created tools.
Core tools include web_search, file_controller, browser_control, computer_control, and many others.
Dynamic tools are stored in the actions/dynamic/ directory and are automatically discovered at runtime.

Chapter 4: Self-Evolving Capabilities
The create_new_skill tool allows IP Prime to write brand new Python tools at runtime.
It generates Python code, validates it with compile(), writes it to the dynamic tools folder,
and registers it immediately without requiring a restart. This is inspired by the OpenViking
self-evolving architecture concept.

Chapter 5: File Processing and RAG
The file_processor module handles all file operations including image processing, PDF extraction,
document summarization, code analysis, and more. It also includes a local RAG (Retrieval Augmented
Generation) system that can index documents into chunks, generate summaries and keywords for each
chunk, and later search the index to retrieve the most relevant context snippets.

Chapter 6: Planner and Executor
The planner uses Gemini AI to decompose user requests into step-by-step action plans.
Each step specifies a tool name, parameters, and whether it is critical. The executor then
runs each step sequentially, passing results between steps and handling errors gracefully.

Chapter 7: Future Roadmap
Planned features include multi-agent collaboration, persistent task queues across sessions,
advanced screen understanding with vision models, and integration with external APIs and services.
The goal is to make IP Prime a fully autonomous desktop operating system assistant.
chunks.summary: This document outlines the architecture of IP Prime, a voice-controlled desktop assistant, detailing its modular design, memory system, skill management, self-evolving capabilities, and file processing.
